tree.setup: Outside the train station, late afternoon. Taxis queue along the curb. You climb into one driven by Uncle Gao, who has a dashboard covered in good-luck charms and a small photo of his granddaughter taped to the sun visor. He drives slowly and carefully, which is unusual for a taxi driver, and he knows it.
tree.setup_zh: 火车站外，傍晚时分。出租车沿着路边排队。你上了高叔叔的车，仪表盘上全是吉祥物，遮阳板上贴着孙女的小照片。他开车很慢很小心——对出租车司机来说不太常见，他自己也知道。

options.feedback: You show him the address on your phone. Uncle Gao puts on reading glasses, studies it carefully, and nods.「认识，走了。」请 + verb is polite and clear.
Uncle Gao asks which one -- there are several in this area. He waits patiently while you check your phone. Short and functional, but he needs more detail.
Uncle Gao smiles politely but he still doesn't know WHERE you're going. Distance alone doesn't help. Try telling him the actual place name.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de. nǐ shì lái wán de ma?
turns.text_en: OK. Are you here for fun?

options.text_pinyin: shì de, dì yī cì lái.
options.text_en: Yes, it's my first time here.
options.feedback: Uncle Gao lights up and begins a gentle tour-guide narration of every building you pass.「那个是老图书馆……」第一次 means 'first time' -- it often unlocks free local advice.
Uncle Gao nods with hometown pride. He was born in this city and has been driving its streets for thirty years. A compliment about his city goes straight to his heart.
Uncle Gao asks what kind of work with genuine curiosity. Taxi drivers in China are often excellent conversationalists -- if you let them.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 到了！二十三块。欢迎来我们城市！
turns.text_pinyin: dào le! èrshísān kuài. huānyíng lái wǒmen chéngshì!
turns.text_en: We're here! Twenty-three yuan. Welcome to our city!

tree.cultural_note: Chinese taxi drivers often chat with passengers -- asking where you're from, if you like the city, and recommending local food. It's part of the ride. Tips are not expected, but a friendly conversation is always appreciated.
